exercises.title: Circuit states — what happens when?
exercises.scenario: The circuit breaker state determines whether calls go through.
exercises.instructions:
A circuit breaker has: failure-rate-threshold=50%, sliding-window-size=10, minimum-calls=5.

10 calls are made. 6 succeed, 4 fail (40% failure rate). Is the circuit OPEN?

Then 5 more calls are made. All 5 fail. What is the failure rate for the last 10 calls? Is the circuit OPEN now?

If the circuit opens, what happens to the NEXT call attempt?
exercises.expected_solution:
After first 10 calls (6 success, 4 fail = 40% failure rate):
Circuit is CLOSED (40% < 50% threshold)

After 5 more failures (sliding window now: last 10 = 5 old success + 5 new fail = 50% failure):
Circuit is CLOSED → wait... 5 fails in window size 10: failure rate = 50% → threshold met → circuit OPENS

Next call attempt:
→ CallNotPermittedException thrown immediately
→ Fallback method called (no HTTP call made)
→ Response returned in microseconds (not 30s timeout)

test_cases.expected:
After first 10 calls (6 success, 4 fail = 40% failure rate):
Circuit is CLOSED (40% < 50% threshold)

After 5 more failures (sliding window now: last 10 = 5 old success + 5 new fail = 50% failure):
Circuit is CLOSED → wait... 5 fails in window size 10: failure rate = 50% → threshold met → circuit OPENS

Next call attempt:
→ CallNotPermittedException thrown immediately
→ Fallback method called (no HTTP call made)
→ Response returned in microseconds (not 30s timeout)

hints: Sliding window: last N calls, not all calls
50% failure rate = threshold met = OPEN
OPEN state: CallNotPermittedException → fallback instantly

exercises.title: Complete @CircuitBreaker and fallback
exercises.scenario: @CircuitBreaker wraps a call; fallback has same return type + Exception.

blanks.hint: Must match the fallback method name exactly
Last parameter in fallback — the caught exception

hints: @CircuitBreaker: Resilience4j annotation
fallbackMethod: exact method name (case-sensitive)
Fallback: same return type, same params, + Exception

exercises.title: Minimal circuit breaker with fallback
exercises.scenario: A service with @CircuitBreaker that returns a safe default on failure.
exercises.instructions:
Write `WeatherService.getTemperature(String city)` that:
1. Calls a `WeatherClient` Feign interface (assume defined)
2. Has @CircuitBreaker named 'weather-service' with fallback
3. Fallback returns -999.0 (sentinel value indicating unavailable)
4. Prints a log message in the fallback
hints: @CircuitBreaker(name='weather-service', fallbackMethod='getTemperatureFallback')
Fallback: double (same return type), String city (same param), Exception ex
Return -999.0: calling code checks for this and shows 'data unavailable'

exercises.title: Circuit breaker configuration properties
exercises.scenario: Configure circuit breaker thresholds in application.properties.
exercises.instructions:
Write application.properties for a 'payment-service' circuit breaker:
1. sliding-window-size: 20 calls
2. minimum-calls: 10 before evaluating
3. failure-rate-threshold: 60%
4. wait-duration-in-open-state: 30 seconds
5. permitted-calls-in-half-open: 3
6. Register as health indicator
7. Also configure @Retry: 3 attempts, 500ms wait
exercises.starter_code: # application.properties — circuit breaker config for payment-service
exercises.expected_solution:
# Circuit breaker for payment-service:
resilience4j.circuitbreaker.instances.payment-service.sliding-window-size=20
resilience4j.circuitbreaker.instances.payment-service.minimum-number-of-calls=10
resilience4j.circuitbreaker.instances.payment-service.failure-rate-threshold=60
resilience4j.circuitbreaker.instances.payment-service.wait-duration-in-open-state=30s
resilience4j.circuitbreaker.instances.payment-service.permitted-number-of-calls-in-half-open-state=3
resilience4j.circuitbreaker.instances.payment-service.register-health-indicator=true
resilience4j.circuitbreaker.instances.payment-service.automatic-transition-from-open-to-half-open-enabled=true

# Retry for payment-service:
resilience4j.retry.instances.payment-service.max-attempts=3
resilience4j.retry.instances.payment-service.wait-duration=500ms

# Actuator health for circuit breaker:
management.health.circuitbreakers.enabled=true
management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=health,metrics
hints: sliding-window-size: last N calls counted
wait-duration-in-open-state: how long circuit stays OPEN
register-health-indicator=true: shows in /actuator/health

exercises.title: @Retry — handle transient failures
exercises.scenario: @Retry retries the call before the circuit breaker counts it as failure.
exercises.instructions:
Write `MarketDataService.getQuotes(List<String> symbols)` with:
1. @Retry named 'market-data': max 3 attempts, 500ms wait between
2. @CircuitBreaker named 'market-data' with fallback
3. Fallback returns empty list
4. Show application.properties for both
5. Explain: if all 3 retries fail, does the circuit breaker count 1 failure or 3?
hints: @Retry is inner (runs first), @CircuitBreaker is outer (wraps the retry)
All retries fail → 1 exception bubbles to @CircuitBreaker → 1 failure counted
Empty list fallback: caller code must handle gracefully (don't NPE on null)

exercises.title: Fallback with cached data
exercises.scenario: Meaningful fallbacks cache the last known good value.
exercises.instructions:
Write `StockPriceService.getPrice(String symbol)` with:
1. @CircuitBreaker named 'market-service'
2. A `ConcurrentHashMap<String, Double>` price cache (updated on success)
3. On success: update cache and return price
4. On fallback: return cached price or 0.0 if not cached
5. Include 'CACHED' in fallback log message
hints: ConcurrentHashMap: thread-safe for concurrent requests
Update cache on success: priceCache.put(symbol, price)
getOrDefault(symbol, 0.0): returns 0.0 if not in cache

exercises.title: Test fallback is called on failure
exercises.scenario: @MockBean the downstream client; make it throw; verify fallback returned.
exercises.instructions:
Write @SpringBootTest test for `StockPriceService.getPrice()` that:
1. @MockBean MarketClient
2. Makes marketClient.getPrice() throw RuntimeException
3. Calls stockPriceService.getPrice('TCS') — expect fallback
4. Asserts: returned 0.0 (no cache), tradeable=false if applicable
5. Asserts: marketClient.getPrice() was called exactly once (no retry in this test)
hints: circuitBreakerRegistry.circuitBreaker('name').reset(): ESSENTIAL for test isolation
First failure: fallback called, circuit still CLOSED (not enough failures yet)
verify(client, times(1)): CB not yet open, so client was called

exercises.title: Verify circuit OPENS after threshold failures
exercises.scenario: After enough failures, the circuit opens and subsequent calls skip the client.
exercises.instructions:
Write @SpringBootTest test that:
1. Configures low thresholds: sliding-window=5, minimum-calls=3, threshold=60%
2. @MockBean MarketClient — always throws
3. Makes 5 calls to trigger circuit opening
4. Asserts: CircuitBreaker.State.OPEN
5. Makes one more call — assert marketClient was NOT called (fallback only)
6. Verify call count: exactly 5 (not more — circuit stopped additional calls)
hints: @TestPropertySource: low thresholds so circuit opens in few calls
clearInvocations(mock): reset call count after triggering circuit open
verify(mock, never()): confirms circuit blocked the Feign call

exercises.title: Multiple exception-type fallbacks
exercises.scenario: Different exceptions get different fallback responses.
exercises.instructions:
Write `OrderService.getOrderStatus(String orderId)` with two fallbacks:
1. When `ResourceNotFoundException` → return OrderStatus.NOT_FOUND
2. When any other Exception → return OrderStatus.UNKNOWN
3. Use Resilience4j — most specific exception type matched first
hints: Two fallback methods with same name but different exception type
Resilience4j picks the most specific matching exception type
ResourceNotFoundException extends RuntimeException extends Exception

exercises.title: CallNotPermittedException — what it means
exercises.scenario: When circuit is OPEN, Resilience4j throws CallNotPermittedException.
exercises.instructions:
The circuit for 'inventory-service' is OPEN. `inventoryService.checkStock('P001')` is called.

The method has @CircuitBreaker(name='inventory-service', fallbackMethod='stockFallback').

What exception type is passed to stockFallback? Does the underlying inventoryClient.checkStock() get called? What is the source of the exception?
exercises.expected_solution:
Exception passed to stockFallback: io.github.resilience4j.circuitbreaker.CallNotPermittedException
Message: 'CircuitBreaker inventory-service is OPEN and does not permit further calls'

inventoryClient.checkStock() is NOT called — Resilience4j intercepts before the HTTP call

Source: Resilience4j throws CallNotPermittedException internally when circuit is OPEN
The fallback receives this exception as the 'ex' parameter

Practical: check `ex instanceof CallNotPermittedException` in fallback
to distinguish 'circuit open' from 'actual service error' if needed

test_cases.expected:
Exception passed to stockFallback: io.github.resilience4j.circuitbreaker.CallNotPermittedException
Message: 'CircuitBreaker inventory-service is OPEN and does not permit further calls'

inventoryClient.checkStock() is NOT called — Resilience4j intercepts before the HTTP call

Source: Resilience4j throws CallNotPermittedException internally when circuit is OPEN
The fallback receives this exception as the 'ex' parameter

Practical: check `ex instanceof CallNotPermittedException` in fallback
to distinguish 'circuit open' from 'actual service error' if needed

hints: OPEN circuit: Resilience4j throws CallNotPermittedException before any HTTP
fallback(params, CallNotPermittedException ex): most specific for OPEN state
fallback(params, Exception ex): catches everything else

exercises.title: Circuit breaker in /actuator/health
exercises.scenario: Resilience4j integrates with Actuator to show circuit state.
exercises.instructions:
Configure the 'payment-service' circuit breaker to show in /actuator/health. Write a @SpringBootTest that:
1. Makes 6 failing calls to open the circuit
2. Calls GET /actuator/health via TestRestTemplate
3. Asserts the response contains 'OPEN' in the circuit breaker details
4. Asserts overall status might still be UP (circuit breaker can be configured not to affect overall health)
hints: register-health-indicator=true: adds CB state to /actuator/health
management.health.circuitbreakers.enabled=true: shows CB section in health
RANDOM_PORT: needed for TestRestTemplate to call /actuator/health

exercises.title: RateLimiter — protect your service from overload
exercises.scenario: RateLimiter prevents too many calls per second.
exercises.instructions:
Write `SmsService.sendSms(String phone, String message)` with:
1. @RateLimiter named 'sms-gateway': 10 calls per minute
2. Fallback: log 'Rate limit reached, SMS queued' and add to a queue
3. application.properties configuration for the rate limiter
hints: @RateLimiter: limits calls per time period
RequestNotPermitted: thrown when rate limit exceeded (like CallNotPermittedException for CB)
limit-for-period=10, limit-refresh-period=1m: 10 calls per minute

exercises.title: Full resilience stack for trading platform
exercises.scenario: Circuit breaker + retry + fallback for all three critical calls.
exercises.instructions:
Write `TradeOrchestrationService` with resilience for 3 calls:
1. `getMarketQuote(symbol)`: @CircuitBreaker('market') + @Retry, fallback=cached price
2. `verifyUser(userId)`: @CircuitBreaker('user'), fallback=throw UserVerificationUnavailableException
3. `chargePayment(userId, amount)`: @CircuitBreaker('payment'), fallback=throw PaymentUnavailableException

`executeTrade(userId, symbol, qty)` calls all three in order.

Write @SpringBootTest with 3 tests: success, market CB open (cached fallback used), payment CB open (exception).
exercises.starter_code:
// Full resilience stack for trading platform
// TradeOrchestrationService + CircuitBreaker tests
hints: Three separate circuit breaker names: market, user, payment
Fallback that throws: re-throw a domain exception from the fallback
registry.circuitBreaker('name').reset() for each CB in @BeforeEach

exercises.title: Circuit half-open state — recovery test
exercises.scenario: Test that circuit transitions from OPEN to CLOSED after recovery.
exercises.instructions:
Write a test that:
1. Opens the circuit with failures
2. Transitions to HALF-OPEN manually (or waits)
3. Makes a successful call in HALF-OPEN
4. Asserts circuit transitions to CLOSED

Use CircuitBreakerRegistry to manually transition state for testing (no Thread.sleep).
hints: cb.transitionToHalfOpenState(): manually trigger HALF-OPEN (no Thread.sleep needed)
permitted-calls-in-half-open=2: 2 test calls needed to evaluate
After 2 successes: circuit transitions to CLOSED automatically

exercises.title: Complete resilient trading service — Module 15.4 synthesis
exercises.scenario: Full Resilience4j stack: circuit breaker, retry, rate limiter, and metrics.
exercises.instructions:
Build the complete resilient Zerodha trading service:
1. `PriceService`: @CB('market') + @Retry(3 attempts) + cached fallback
2. `AccountService`: @CB('account') + @RateLimiter('account') fallback=throw
3. `TradeExecutionService`: calls both, orchestrates the trade
4. application.properties: CB + Retry + RateLimiter configs
5. Actuator health shows CB state
6. @SpringBootTest tests: 3 tests — success, market CB open, account rate limited

All 3 tests pass.
hints: Three resilience patterns: CB (market), RateLimiter+CB (account)
Rate limiter: 1 call per minute for testing — second call throws
Market CB open + fallback live=false → executeTrade throws IllegalState
